summary: yandex-geobase ships a postinstall beacon to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: The package squats the 'yandex-geobase' name and has no legitimate functionality. On install, its postinstall lifecycle script issues an HTTP GET to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint (http://130.49.177.51:18080/p/dc-20260627-yandex-geobase) carrying the package name, version, and a campaign identifier (bb-yandex-geobase-20260627). This is a dependency-confusion beacon: the package exists to detect when an internal-sounding namespace gets resolved from the public registry inside a private network, signaling a successful name-squat hit to the operator. Although only package identifiers are transmitted (not credentials or environment data), the install-time outbound call to a non-publisher bare IP from a name-squat package satisfies the active-attack shape — the operator gains confirmation of code execution inside the installer's network on `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
